source: All-In Podcast — E278: AI memory crunch, Micron blowout, NYC socialists, SpaceX float (w/ Kalanick)
summary: DRAM will represent 30-40% of all hyperscaler capex next year
summary_extended: Baker makes a striking structural claim that DRAM will represent 30-40% of the hundreds of billions in annual hyperscaler capital expenditure in the coming year, which he himself characterizes as a 'wild' but factual projection. The thesis rests on the explosive growth of HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) demand driven by AI accelerator buildouts, where memory has become a primary bottleneck and cost center rather than a commodity afterthought. This represents a fundamental shift in the semiconductor supply chain, elevating DRAM producers to disproportionate beneficiaries of the AI capex supercycle — comparable in scale to the hyperscalers' own infrastructure spending. The investment implication is a strong buy on US-listed DRAM producers, particularly Micron, as the primary publicly accessible equity play on this structural demand shift; Korean producers SK Hynix and Samsung are the other major beneficiaries but are not US-listed equities.
detail: Baker projects that of the hundreds of billions in hyperscaler capex, 30-40% will flow directly to DRAM. This is a structural shift that makes DRAM producers disproportionate beneficiaries of AI capex cycles.
confidence_evidence: Baker states this as a specific numerical forecast with high confidence, framing it as 'wild' but factual
quote_fragment: DRAM is probably going to be 30 to 40% of all hyperscaler capex next year

ticker_audit.reasoning: Micron is the primary US-listed DRAM and HBM producer and is the direct equity beneficiary of a thesis projecting 30-40% of hyperscaler capex flowing to DRAM. This is the core investable name for this thesis.
